conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 13 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and you're responsible for things like his day-to-day care, food, clothing, and pocket money?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for all his day-to-day care - I look after his meals, buy his clothes, and cover all his needs like school things and pocket money.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status. Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here? For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or are you working legally in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who might also be eligible to claim for him (such as another parent or guardian he also lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - he lives with me and I'm the one looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Is Alex currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in any form of local authority care, detention, or residential educational accommodation funded by a local authority?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of that applies. Alex isn't in hospital or in any kind of care. He just lives at home with me and goes to school normally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 13 years old (under 16, so meets the age requirement)
✓ You are responsible for Alex - he lives with you and you provide his day-to-day care
✓ You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ Alex is not in hospital long-term or in local authority care

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can go ahead and apply for it if you haven't already done so.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex, meaning they live with him and are responsible for his day-to-day care including meals, clothing, and pocket money. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital for an extended period or in local authority care. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. All key criteria are satisfied: Alex is under 16 years old, the claimant is responsible for him and he lives with them, the claimant has the right to reside in the UK, no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospitalization or care) that would affect eligibility.
